title: FAQ split accordion
description: An introduction and support action beside an interactive accordion.

selection.description: Use when frequently asked questions need context, reassurance, and a nearby escalation path.
queries: Add an FAQ beside introductory copy
Create a support-oriented accordion section
avoidWhen: There are fewer than three questions or every answer must remain visible.

responsive.compact: Stack the introduction before the full-width accordion.
responsive.wide: Place the introduction and accordion in asymmetric columns.

title: FAQ stacked list
description: A heading followed by an always-visible semantic question-and-answer list.

selection.description: Use when answers are short, highly scannable, and useful to compare without interaction.
queries: Show all FAQs in a simple list
Create a compact questions and answers section
avoidWhen: Answers are long enough to make the page difficult to scan.

responsive.compact: Keep each answer in a single stacked reading flow.
responsive.wide: Retain one readable column or use a restrained two-column definition list.

title: Services icon grid
description: A concise service or capability grid with optional icons and per-item actions.

selection.description: Use when several offers need equal visual weight and brief explanations.
queries: Show services in an icon grid
Create an equal-weight capability section
avoidWhen: One service is dominant enough to require a featured media treatment.

responsive.compact: Collapse cards to one column with icons aligned consistently.
responsive.wide: Distribute cards across two to four equal columns.

title: Services media grid
description: A collection of service summaries paired with project-owned imagery.

selection.description: Use when services benefit from visual examples, outcomes, or environmental context.
queries: Create image-led service cards
Show service offerings with media
avoidWhen: Imagery is generic or would distract from a short utility-oriented list.

responsive.compact: Stack media above copy and keep cards in a single column.
responsive.wide: Use a balanced two- or three-column collection with consistent media ratios.

title: Process numbered steps
description: A linear sequence that explains how work progresses from first action to outcome.

selection.description: Use for short, ordered processes where each step has similar importance and amount of copy.
queries: Explain our process in numbered steps
Create a simple how-it-works section
avoidWhen: The sequence branches, loops, or depends on application state.

responsive.compact: Stack steps vertically and preserve their explicit order.
responsive.wide: Arrange steps in a row or restrained grid with visible sequence markers.

title: Timeline alternating media
description: A chronological or narrative sequence with optional media alternating beside each entry.

selection.description: Use for histories, journeys, milestones, and longer processes where each stage deserves visual space.
queries: Build an alternating timeline
Tell our journey with milestones and images
avoidWhen: The content is not genuinely sequential or has fewer than three meaningful stages.

responsive.compact: Use one chronological column with media before its related copy.
responsive.wide: Alternate media and copy while keeping source order chronological.

title: Proof metric strip
description: A compact row of outcome metrics with labels and optional supporting context.

selection.description: Use when three to five credible numbers can quickly establish reach, quality, or impact.
queries: Add an impact statistics strip
Show key proof points in one row
avoidWhen: Numbers lack a clear definition, source, or meaningful unit.

responsive.compact: Wrap metrics into readable rows without shrinking values below legibility.
responsive.wide: Keep metrics in a single balanced strip where space allows.

title: Testimonials editorial list
description: A quote-led list that gives each testimonial enough room for context and attribution.

selection.description: Use when a small set of detailed testimonials is more persuasive than a dense carousel.
queries: Create an editorial testimonial section
Show detailed client quotes with outcomes
avoidWhen: Quotes are anonymous, repetitive, or too short to need editorial treatment.

responsive.compact: Stack quotes and keep attribution immediately after each quotation.
responsive.wide: Use an editorial list or restrained columns without changing reading order.

title: Partners logo wall
description: A labeled wall of partner, sponsor, client, or press marks.

selection.description: Use when recognizable organizations provide meaningful trust or demonstrate ecosystem reach.
queries: Add a partner logo wall
Show sponsors and press marks
avoidWhen: The marks are decorative, outdated, or lack permission to display.

responsive.compact: Wrap marks into a two-column grid with consistent cells.
responsive.wide: Use four to six columns while preserving each mark's intrinsic ratio.

title: Gallery featured grid
description: A featured visual followed by a supporting image or project grid.

selection.description: Use when one image or project should lead while the remaining collection stays browsable.
queries: Build a gallery with one featured image
Create a portfolio grid with a lead project
avoidWhen: Every item must receive identical prominence or the collection has fewer than three items.

responsive.compact: Stack the selected image and horizontally scrollable or wrapped thumbnails.
responsive.wide: Give the selected image dominant space beside or above supporting thumbnails.

title: Stories featured plus grid
description: A featured article or story followed by a compact archive grid.

selection.description: Use when editorial content needs a clear lead story and several secondary routes.
queries: Create a featured story with article cards
Build a news or case-study landing section
avoidWhen: All records are utility results that should be sorted or filtered instead.

responsive.compact: Stack the lead story before the archive and keep metadata concise.
responsive.wide: Give the lead story a wider column than supporting cards.

title: People profile grid
description: A team, board, stylist, instructor, or contributor grid with role context.

selection.description: Use when individual people and their roles are important to trust, selection, or organizational transparency.
queries: Add a team profile grid
Show stylists or board members
avoidWhen: Names are incidental or profiles have no useful role or biography context.

responsive.compact: Use one or two columns and keep names adjacent to their roles.
responsive.wide: Use three or four columns with consistent portrait ratios.

title: Events featured list
description: A lead event followed by upcoming dates with clear time, place, and action information.

selection.description: Use when one event deserves emphasis while visitors also need to scan upcoming opportunities.
queries: Create a featured event and upcoming list
Show community events with dates and locations
avoidWhen: The page needs full calendar navigation or availability scheduling.

responsive.compact: Stack events and keep date, time, and location in a predictable order.
responsive.wide: Place the featured event beside a compact upcoming list.

title: Newsletter inline band
description: A compact value proposition and email form arranged as one conversion band.

selection.description: Use when email capture supports the page's content or community relationship without requiring a dedicated landing page.
queries: Add an inline newsletter signup
Create an email capture band
avoidWhen: Consent, segmentation, or onboarding requires a long multi-field form.

responsive.compact: Stack copy, form field, and submit action with full-width controls.
responsive.wide: Place copy beside an inline form while retaining a readable consent note.

title: Announcement dismissible
description: A concise announcement with an optional action and accessible dismissal.

selection.description: Use for timely, site-wide information that should be noticed but can be dismissed.
queries: Add a dismissible announcement
Show a temporary promotion with a link
avoidWhen: The message is critical enough that dismissal would hide required information.

responsive.compact: Wrap message and action without obscuring the dismiss control.
responsive.wide: Keep the announcement in one concise horizontal band.

title: Participation pathways
description: A set of clear ways to join, give, volunteer, apply, or otherwise participate.

selection.description: Use when an organization has two or more distinct participation routes with different commitments.
queries: Show ways to get involved
Create giving volunteering and application pathways
avoidWhen: There is only one primary conversion action.

responsive.compact: Stack pathways and keep each action adjacent to its commitment description.
responsive.wide: Use equal columns unless one pathway is intentionally primary.

title: Location contact cards
description: A set of address, hours, contact, and optional direction cards for physical locations.

selection.description: Use when visitors need to compare or act on one or more physical locations.
queries: Show business locations and hours
Create contact cards with addresses and directions
avoidWhen: The business is fully remote or a single address belongs directly inside a larger contact composition.

responsive.compact: Stack location cards and preserve address line breaks.
responsive.wide: Use equal columns and align hours and actions consistently.

title: Contact form split
description: A contact introduction and direct contact routes paired with a focused inquiry form.

selection.description: Use when the page should convert interest into a structured inquiry while keeping direct email or phone routes visible.
queries: Create a contact section with an inquiry form
Add a split contact form with direct contact details
avoidWhen: The flow requires authentication, payment, scheduling, or a long multi-step application.

responsive.compact: Stack the introduction, direct contact routes, fields, and submit action in reading order.
responsive.wide: Place concise contact context beside a focused form without separating labels from controls.

title: Content media split
description: A general-purpose text and image section with reversible media placement and an optional action.

selection.description: Use for flexible about, feature, program, place, product, or service narratives that need one supporting image.
queries: Create a text and image split section
Add an about section with an image and action
avoidWhen: Several equal items need collection semantics or the image is the primary story rather than supporting context.

responsive.compact: Stack copy before media regardless of the wide-screen media position so reading order remains predictable.
responsive.wide: Place copy and media in balanced columns and allow the project to choose which side leads.

title: Content media stacked
description: A wide featured image followed by adaptable editorial copy and an optional action.

selection.description: Use when a place, project, campaign, or editorial moment needs a wide image to establish context before the supporting text.
queries: Create a wide image with text below
Add a featured editorial image and copy section
avoidWhen: The image is merely decorative or the copy and media need equal side-by-side weight.

responsive.compact: Keep the image, copy, and action in a single readable column.
responsive.wide: Use a wide media frame with copy and action aligned beneath it.

title: Confirmation next actions
description: A success or completion state with a clear summary and relevant next actions.

selection.description: Use after submissions, purchases, signups, or guided flows to confirm the result and prevent a dead end.
queries: Create a thank-you confirmation section
Show success details and next steps
avoidWhen: The operation has not completed or its status is still uncertain.

responsive.compact: Stack actions and keep the confirmation summary concise.
responsive.wide: Center the outcome or place the summary beside a compact action group.

title: Product collection
description: A browsable product grid with imagery, price, concise context, and a direct product action.

selection.description: Use for shop, catalog, merchandise, or plan collections where comparable products need equal visual weight.
queries: Create a shop product grid
Show a collection of products with prices
avoidWhen: The items are editorial stories or only one product needs a detailed purchase flow.

responsive.compact: Stack products in one readable column.
responsive.wide: Present products in a balanced multi-column grid.

title: Product detail gallery
description: A product purchase surface combining an accessible gallery, options, quantity, price, and primary action.

selection.description: Use when one physical or configurable product needs visual inspection and a focused purchase decision.
queries: Create a product detail page section
Add product images, variants, quantity, and add to cart
avoidWhen: The offer has no visual or configurable details, or several products need comparison first.

responsive.compact: Stack the gallery before purchase controls.
responsive.wide: Keep the gallery and purchase panel in adjacent columns.

title: Cart checkout summary
description: An editable cart line-item list beside a transparent order summary and checkout action.

selection.description: Use when shoppers must review quantities, pricing, discounts, and totals before checkout.
queries: Create a shopping cart summary
Show editable cart items and checkout totals
avoidWhen: The purchase is complete or there is no editable cart state.

responsive.compact: Stack the summary after the cart items.
responsive.wide: Keep totals visible beside the item list.

title: Multi-step selection flow
description: A reusable guided decision flow that divides a complex selection into clear, reviewable steps.

selection.description: Use when a recommendation or result depends on several sequential choices.
queries: Create a guided product selector
Build a multi-step recommendation flow
avoidWhen: A single choice or short form can complete the task without sequencing.

responsive.compact: Stack progress and choices with full-width controls.
responsive.wide: Use the available width for comparable choice cards.

title: Estimate flow
description: A guided scope and timing workflow ending in a transparent planning range.

selection.description: Use when service visitors benefit from a directional estimate before speaking with the team.
queries: Create a service price estimator
Build a quote flow with a price range
avoidWhen: Pricing is fixed and can be shown directly or the estimate would be misleading without consultation.

responsive.compact: Stack choice cards and keep the estimate readable.
responsive.wide: Compare options across columns within each step.

title: Booking flow
description: A staged service, date, time, and contact-detail booking workflow.

selection.description: Use when a visitor must choose an appointment type and available schedule before confirmation.
queries: Create an appointment booking flow
Build service, date, and time selection
avoidWhen: Availability is not known or the only action is an external booking link.

responsive.compact: Use one choice column and modal date selection.
responsive.wide: Let service and time choices use compact grids.

title: Filtered results master detail
description: Search and facets paired with a result list and persistent detail pane.

selection.description: Use for directories, inventories, programs, jobs, or knowledge bases where visitors compare results without losing context.
queries: Create a searchable directory with details
Build filtered results with a master detail layout
avoidWhen: Results are few enough to scan without filters or every result needs a full page.

responsive.compact: Stack the active result below or switch between list and detail panes.
responsive.wide: Keep results and selected detail visible together.

title: Authentication shell
description: A focused account-access shell with primary authentication and clear alternate paths.

selection.description: Use for sign-in, account creation, password reset, or invitation acceptance routes.
queries: Create a sign-in page
Build a simple authentication shell
avoidWhen: Authentication is a minor inline action or belongs to a third-party hosted page.

responsive.compact: Use a full-width panel with comfortable controls.
responsive.wide: Center a restrained authentication panel.

title: Dashboard overview
description: A dashboard opening view with headline metrics, a trend frame, and recent operational records.

selection.description: Use when an application home needs a quick status read before deeper management tasks.
queries: Create an admin dashboard overview
Show metrics, a chart, and recent records
avoidWhen: There is no meaningful overview or users should enter a single focused task directly.

responsive.compact: Stack metrics, chart, and records with horizontal table overflow.
responsive.wide: Pair the trend and recent records beneath a metric strip.

title: Resource index
description: A searchable, sortable, selectable management index with a clear creation action.

selection.description: Use for admin lists where users find, sort, select, and act on structured resources.
queries: Create an admin resource list
Build a searchable data grid index
avoidWhen: The collection is read-only editorial content or has too few records to need management controls.

responsive.compact: Stack controls and allow intentional horizontal grid overflow.
responsive.wide: Keep the header action aligned and expose useful grid columns.

title: Resource editor detail
description: A record-editing surface with contextual actions, a structured form, and secondary metadata.

selection.description: Use when application users need to edit one resource while retaining status and metadata context.
queries: Create an admin edit resource page
Build a record detail editor with tabs
avoidWhen: The task is a short modal edit or the resource is strictly read-only.

responsive.compact: Wrap contextual actions and retain one form column.
responsive.wide: Use tabbed content within a broad editing surface.

title: Horizontal story
description: A deliberately horizontal, scroll-snapping narrative made from ordered media and copy chapters.

selection.description: Use for a finite visual sequence where horizontal progression is part of the editorial idea.
queries: Create a horizontal scrolling story
Build a side-scrolling sequence of visual chapters
avoidWhen: The content is a general item carousel, an infinite feed, or horizontal scrolling would obscure essential navigation.

responsive.compact: Keep snap points, readable card widths, and visible overflow cues.
responsive.wide: Reveal the next scene while maintaining a focused chapter viewport.

title: Chaptered presentation
description: A long-form presentation with persistent chapter navigation and substantial media-led sections.

selection.description: Use for case studies, reports, campaign narratives, or presentations that readers may enter and revisit by chapter.
queries: Create a chaptered case study
Build a long-form presentation with anchor navigation
avoidWhen: The story is short enough for one content section or chapters do not have stable linkable identities.

responsive.compact: Place chapter navigation before a single chapter column.
responsive.wide: Keep chapter navigation beside the scrolling presentation.
